segments.title: German torpedo boats favored attack over screening
segments.summary: The episode frames Imperial German torpedo-boat destroyer design as an offensive answer to cruiser shortages, fleet limits, and British numerical pressure.
segments.body: German large torpedo boats carried extra offensive weight because the Kaiserliche Marine lacked the cruisers, capital ships, funding freedom, and numbers needed for prolonged screening fights against the Royal Navy.

evidence.note: The transcript contrasts German boats with British destroyers and ties the offensive bias to cruiser and capital-ship limits.
The transcript explains that sacrificing cruiser construction pushed the ships toward short, torpedo-heavy offensive action.
The transcript says the boats fit strategic need even though Germany also needed stronger skirmishing and screening assets.
